rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign only for output features or declared auxiliary variables.
generic_rules: When resolving validator diagnostics, trace root causes of unresolved references and correct them at the source.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions to avoid type errors.
Minimize repairs to only the components causing validation failures without affecting unrelated architecture.
Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantees must reference valid identifiers or declared auxiliary variables; avoid using undefined or inaccessible names.
Implementation-level AGREE contracts should use assign for outputs and avoid placing guarantees in implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Adding new architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics instead of resolving root reference issues.
Placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes where only assign or output declarations are allowed.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about missing subcomponents
AGREE validator diagnostics about connection end references
Component type vs implementation scope placement rules
